### Post by Master_J on 2006-07-10
After spending two days searching this forum and others, reinstalling Ubuntu while trying to install drivers for mye graphic card I'm about to give up.

I'll just post my Xorg file, and results from different inputs in the terminal.

> # /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"

	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"

I've also installed Compiz, doesn't know if this has something to do with this problem.

Why doesn't my graphic card support direct rendering? And why is my XFree86-DRI missing?

### Post by duk on 2006-07-16

---

### Post by autocrosser on 2006-07-16
Two things---

#1--look at you Xorg.0.log with System log viewer--this will tell you what is being loaded (or not loaded) & a "fair" reason why.

#2--Compiz & XGL tend to turn off DRI (I'm a Nvidia user, so ATI guys can correct me if I'm wrong)--Take a look at your FPS with:
glxgears --printfps  & see what you get.. I had a ATI 9600 Pro & sold it on eBay (bought an Nvidia card).

---
